name: imagefx
description: SIMD separable Gaussian blur for live image effects. Each RGBA pixel is widened to an i32x4 and the multiply-accumulate over the (2r+1)-tap kernel runs 4 channels at once; JS has no vector type and processes one channel at a time.

alloc.doc: reserve byteLen bytes (8-byte aligned), growing linear memory; returns byte ptr

blur.doc: separable Gaussian blur src->tmp (horizontal) then tmp->dst (vertical); clamp-to-edge; out = clamp((sum*recip)>>shift, 0, 255)

memory.name: src
memory.note: RGBA source; 16-byte slack so 16-byte SIMD loads never over-read past memory

notes: Integer fixed-point math (weights + recip/shift) makes the WASM output byte-identical to a scalar-JS twin. Image rasterization (decode/scale) is done by the native canvas; only the blur runs in WASM. src/tmp/dst must be distinct buffers.
